query: 请基于 minsky86.pdf 中 Minsky 的 Society of Mind 论文，用中文解释：Society of Mind、agents、K-lines、frames 之间是什么关系？这种由许多简单 agent 协作形成智能的机制，与现代软件 agent 的规划和工具/检索机制有什么可类比之处？

answer_blocks.text: 根据提供的检索材料，我无法找到关于 Minsky 的《Society of Mind》论文中 agents、K-lines、frames 等概念的解释及相关关系，也无法进行其与现代软件 agent 机制的类比分析。检索到的内容均为经济学相关内容，与您所询问的认知科学或人工智能理论无关。
建议您检查所提供的文档来源（minsky86.pdf），或者提供一个明确包含《Society of Mind》相关内容的文件，以便我进行准确的回答。

citations.content:
will be extended to include more than the skeletal relations. In the more complete case, the government budget position will be seen to affect relative prices, money prices, and the movement of prices. The money supply does not appear in the price level equation; the quantity theory is not visible. Money appears in the subsystems that determine realized investment and the financing of government deficits. In particular, money affects total demand and the course of prices through the banking mechanisms that finance activity and control over capital and financial assets.

[Image: 1939cc94b0366914e56f3a2e7989bc06993863bf4177c9d19576d7e390153edf.jpg]

various items of overhead, ancillary, and capital costs. Each of these average cost curves, in turn, has its minimum point on the unique marginal cost curve. These average and marginal cost curves are sketched in Figure 7.2.

[Image: e959508787bca33ebb2f23aed00f05c512b0154724c45adbd5296cf16820462c.jpg]
citations.preview: various items of overhead, ancillary, and capital costs. Each of these average cost curves, in turn,

citations.content:
Therefore, there exists a functional relation between the price, $\mathbf{P}_{\mathbf{K}}$ , of a particular or a representative capital or financial asset and the quantity of money, M. Normally the price of a capital asset is a rising function of the quantity of money, for as the quantity increases the value of the insurance embodied in money decreases. As the price of money is always one, this implies that the price level of income-yielding capital assets increases. Furthermore, the functional relation has a logarithmic shape unless:

[Image: 4d00c23fdcbbb64ad6aa1e3d5ca9a7c10531627aabdbad1922c8221346d77054.jpg]
citations.preview: Therefore, there exists a functional relation between the price, $\mathbf{P}_{\mathbf{K}}$ , of a pa

citations.content:
Figures 4.5 and 4.6 show data for households; Figure 4.5 the ratio of liabilities to personal income and Figure 4.6 the ratio of liabilities to money.

[Image: 20325152a194c5a0cb66221e1e3d0e1b72aea352940cdcf6f5cd754cc937200d.jpg]
citations.preview: Figures 4.5 and 4.6 show data for households; Figure 4.5 the ratio of liabilities to personal income

citations.content:
Price-taking firms react to changes in demand by adjusting output along their marginal cost curve. Even though such firms own and operate capital assets and have debts, they do not have the power to set their price according to what they need to satisfy such costs; instead, they are forced to accept what they can get; they take price as a parameter and set output along their MC curve.

[Image: b553764fa05ab8f6c3542bc63693c1c17ef7b8adbaa39d0f2c0882fed85384b9.jpg]

In particular, money affects total demand and the course of prices through the banking mechanisms that finance activity and control over capital and financial assets. The money supply does not appear in the price level equation; the quantity theory is not visible. various items of overhead, ancillary, and capital costs. Each of these average cost curves, in turn, has its minimum point on the unique marginal cost curve. These average and marginal cost curves are sketched in Figure 7.2. [Image: e959508787bca33ebb2f23aed00f05c512b0154724c45adbd5296cf16820462c.jpg]

Therefore, there exists a functional relation between the price, $\mathbf{P}_{\mathbf{K}}$ , of a particular or a representative capital or financial asset and the quantity of money, M. Normally the price of a capital asset is a rising function of the quantity of money, for as the quantity increases the value of the insurance embodied in money decreases. As the price of money is always one, this implies that the price level of income-yielding capital assets increases. Furthermore, the functional relation has a logarithmic shape unless: [Image: 4d00c23fdcbbb64ad6aa1e3d5ca9a7c10531627aabdbad1922c8221346d77054.jpg]

various items of overhead, ancillary, and capital costs. Each of these average cost curves, in turn, has its minimum point on the unique marginal cost curve. These average and marginal cost curves are sketched in Figure 7.2. [Image: e959508787bca33ebb2f23aed00f05c512b0154724c45adbd5296cf16820462c.jpg]
